title: Treasury Seeks Public Comment on GENIUS Act Notice of Proposed Rulemaking Concerning State-Level Regulatory Regimes
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (NPRM) on April 1, 2026, seeking public comment on broad-based principles for determining whether state-level regulatory regimes for payment stablecoin issuers are "substantially similar" to the federal framework under the GENIUS Act. Issuers with consolidated total outstanding issuance not exceeding $10 billion may opt for state-level regulation if their state regime meets the substantial similarity standard. Comments will be accepted for 60 days following Federal Register publication.
key_points: Treasury issued its first proposed rule to implement the GENIUS Act, requesting public comment on principles for evaluating state regulatory regimes.
Under the GENIUS Act, payment stablecoin issuers with consolidated outstanding issuance of $10 billion or less may opt for state-level regulation if substantially similar to federal framework.
The NPRM establishes broad-based principles for determining substantial similarity between state and federal regulatory frameworks.
This NPRM builds on an advance notice of proposed rulemaking issued in September 2025.
Public comments will be accepted for 60 days after Federal Register publication and will be viewable at regulations.gov.
why_it_matters: This NPRM represents a critical step in establishing the regulatory architecture for payment stablecoins in the U.S. by defining the conditions under which state-regulated issuers can operate under the federal GENIUS Act framework. Market participants, stablecoin issuers, and state regulators will need to understand these principles to determine compliance pathways and competitive implications for the stablecoin market.
macro_context: Stablecoin regulation affects the broader digital asset ecosystem and could influence monetary transmission mechanisms if stablecoins become more widely used in payment systems. The regulatory clarity provided by this framework may affect adoption rates and investor sentiment in crypto markets.
important_numbers: $10,000,000,000 (consolidated total outstanding issuance threshold for state-level regime opt-in)
notes: Source is relevant to macro catalysts as regulatory developments in digital assets can serve as market-moving events. The GENIUS Act implementation represents significant regulatory infrastructure for the stablecoin market. Published date confirmed as April 1, 2026 (9 days before TODAY), making this a recent source. Low market impact confidence due to this being a comment-seeking notice rather than a final rule with immediate market implications.
